title: Latest news, sport and opinion from the Guardian
meta_description: Latest international news, sport and comment from the Guardian
text_content: Middle East crisis Hide Donald Trump and his secretary of state Marco Rubio People look at residential buildings damaged by an Iranian missile strike in Arad, southern Israel A woman looks out from her destroyed apartment in Tehran, Iran Displaced people celebrate Eid al-Fitr in a temporary encampment in Beirut, Lebanon A girl sits on a pallet in a temporary encampment for displaced people in Beirut A tanker off Muscat, Oman Strait of Hormuz Trump tells Iran it has 48 hours to open Hormuz or US will ‘obliterate’ its power plants US president threatens to take out Iranian energy facilities – ‘starting with the biggest one first’ – if Tehran does not reopen the strait Middle East crisis live Iranian official claims strait of Hormuz open to all but ships of ‘enemy’ countries Joe Kent US counter-terrorism chief who resigned says he fears retaliation but has no regrets UK Iran not believed to have capability or intent to bomb Britain, say minister Middle East crisis live Iranian official claims strait of Hormuz open to all but ships of ‘enemy’ countries Joe Kent US counter-terrorism chief who resigned says he fears retaliation but has no regrets UK Iran not believed to have capability or intent to bomb Britain, say minister Israel Almost 100 wounded in Iranian missile strikes on southern Israel Social media Iran social media strategy pivots to information war amid US-Israel attack News Hide Cuba Cuba’s power grid collapses in third nationwide blackout amid US oil blockade Network breakdowns compounded by daily blackouts of up to 12 hours caused by fuel shortages Sudan Strike on Sudan hospital kills at least 64 and wounds 89 more, WHO reports Donald Trump ‘Vile’ Trump condemned for gloating over Robert Mueller death Robert Mueller Robert Mueller, special counsel who investigated Trump-Russia ties, dies at 81 Brussels attacks ‘It is a shock’: survivors of Brussels bombings face pension cuts 10 years on People who suffered life-changing injuries in 2016 attacks are now fighting deductions in state compensation Slovenia Slovenia goes to polls in election marked by claims of anti-Romany rhetoric US immigration Trump threatens to send ICE to airports on Monday amid DHS funding standoff Trump briefing President says ICE agents at airports would ‘do security like no one has ever seen before’ Hawaii floods Hawaii urges residents to ‘leave now’ amid worst flooding in over 20 years Spain Alabama student reportedly fell to his death in Barcelona waters by accident BTS ‘So awesome’: Fans on K-pop’s biggest comeback Tens of thousands gathered in Seoul to show that, after a four-year hiatus, they had still kept a place for the band in a very changed world The Sunday read Hide ‘The stakes are enormous’: how a prolonged Iran war could shock the global economy Donald Trump’s ‘little excursion’ is likely to have long-term effects, from oil prices to inflation to growth, say experts In focus Hide Italy Meloni v the judges: high stakes for Italian PM in vote on judiciary overhaul Referendum is being seen as a de facto vote on Giorgia Meloni’s government – and the polls are neck and neck Russia-Ukraine war US, Ukrainian negotiators meet in Florida as Russian attacks kill more civilians Donald Trump Trump is eager to disclose details on others’ health – but not his own Features Hide Culture We are living in a period of political anti-intellectualism. But in pop culture, clever is the new cool Television Come at the king … HBO changed TV forever, but is its crown under threat in the age of streaming and Trump? More features Hide BTS BTS review – having lost none of their chemistry, this is a comeback of epic proportions Honest playlist ‘The lyrics to Lola by the Kinks are gross – I was really taken aback’: Moby’s honest playlist Grace Dent on restaurants Osteria Vibrato, London W1: “Worth singing loudly about” – restaurant review Travel Scrambling, walking and swimming in splendid isolation: 75 years of the UK’s national parks Ask Annalisa Barbieri It’s always me who makes the effort to see my friends. Don’t they value me? The moment I knew I was crying and couldn’t stop. When he reassured me, I believed in the future he could see Sport Hide Football live Carabao Cup final buildup, Premier League news, and more – matchday live It’s a big day in the Premier League, plus there’s the Carabao Cup final between Arsenal and Manchester City. Join Emillia Hawkins Latest Newcastle Howe aims to end derby drought and lift gathering gloom Tottenham Tudor pleased Spurs are ‘sleeping less’ as focus sharpens for run-in Nottm Forest Pereira seeks more magic in league showdown at Spurs WSL roundup Shaw’s 13-minute treble edges City closer to title as Chelsea falter Football ‘As long as I’m playing, I’m happy’: O’Reilly revels in role of City’s Mr Versatile The England international has become a key cog for Pep Guardiola’s title chasers and wants more glory in Sunday’s Carabao Cup final Jonathan Wilson Master and apprentice reunite with more than a trophy at stake Arsenal ‘We enjoy it’: Gabriel relishing another battle with Haaland Man City ‘Still a lot to play for’: Doku focuses on final after European exit Arsenal ‘One-nil down, two-one up’: when Gunners won their first League Cup Athletics Kerr goads rival Hocker with ‘night night’ gesture after surging to world indoor gold Motor sport Verstappen denied Nürburgring victory after disqualification for tyre blunder Tennis Serena Williams courts drama once again as game of ‘will she won’t she’ goes on The American last hit a competitive ball in 2022 but Instagram posts show her training again, with her motives still unknown Miami Open Osaka casts doubt on her future after first-round defeat Wimbledon Video reviews will allow players to challenge umpire calls More sport Hide Football European roundup: Bayern hammer Union Berlin to close on records Cycling Milano-Sanremo marred by Silvestri crash as Pogacar pips Pidcock NFL ‘It’s an excuse’: Giants’ Skattebo says CTE and asthma are ‘fake’ Sailing Ratcliffe’s Ineos ‘taking legal steps’ against Ainslie over America’s Cup boat Opinion Hide double quotation mark The Wordle guy’s latest move tells us a lot about modern-day ambition Polly Hudson For some, creating a smash hit puzzle would have been enough to kick back for life. But for the Josh Wardles and Timothée Chalamets of the world, not even the moon is enough double quotation mark It’s easy to miss – but lower courts are actually doing their job in restraining Trump David Kirp At a time when the bad news feels endless, we should celebrate the gutsiness of a judiciary quietly standing up for democracy More opinion Hide It’s time for the UN to formally recognise the transatlantic slavery trade as a crime against humanity John Dramani Mahama double quotation mark Infertility: at a time when we need the right words, some are unable to find them Nuala McGovern double quotation mark How tacking centre left will help Labour win the next election Andy Beckett double quotation mark Israel has crushed Unrwa in Gaza – and the rest of the world has done nothing Philippe Lazzarini Editorials Hide The Guardian view Anonymity in art: the ‘unmasking’ of Banksy and Ferrante should stop The Guardian view Meningitis in Kent: we must not take public health systems for granted Cartoon Hide Natalya Lobanova Quitting social media I’ve left Meta and Insta behind. What could possibly go wrong? More top stories Hide South Korea At least 14 people killed in fire at South Korean car parts factory Almost 60 injured in blaze in Daejeon with footage seemingly showing people jumping from burning building to escape France Mayoral races in balance as voting opens in last round of French local elections Justin Timberlake Justin Timberlake’s DWI arrest video is released despite his attempt to block it Afghanistan ‘This is the saddest moment’: families search for loved ones on Eid after Kabul hospital strike Petrol prices Six fuel ships bound for Australia cancelled as Bowen concedes ‘flow of oil to Asian refineries has slowed’ Climate crisis & environment Hide Solar power ‘It was our little idyll – until the solar farm landed’: the battle raging in the heart of the British countryside Electric vehicles US interest in electric vehicles surges as gas prices jump amid Iran war Mexico Mexico’s monarch butterfly population jumps 64%, offering hope for at-risk species Hawaii More than 230 people rescued as flash flooding hits Hawaii and fears dam could fail UK news Hide Conservatives Tory chief whip reposts AI video created by far-right figure who was jailed for hate crimes House of Lords Evgeny Lebedev and Ian Botham have lowest Lords attendance, records show Carer's allowance MPs threaten fresh inquiry into carers allowance scandal amid redress delays Violence against women and girls Family courts in England and Wales ‘not good enough’ for women and children, minister says World news Hide US Excommunicated Catholic deacon still waiting on overdue appeal outcome after his son was molested by priest Art Death, power and paranoia: painting that shocked German society finally returns to Berlin Oil US lifts sanctions on Iranian oil at sea in bid to ease supply pressures
